raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this formal complaint against Citibank ( XXXX XXXX ) for violating the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) and Regulation Z by failing to conduct a reasonable investigation into a documented billing error.

Under Regulation Z, 12 CFR 1026.13 ( a ) ( 3 ), a billing error includes charges for goods " not delivered to the consumer or the consumer 's designee as agreed. '' On XX/XX/year>, my account ( card ending in XXXX ) was charged {$310.00} by the merchant listed as " XXXX XXXX * XXXX XXXX XXXX CA XXXX '' I purchased a XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, but the seller committed mail fraud using a " fake tracking '' scheme. They shipped a XXXX XXXX  decoy envelope to a different address in my zip code to generate a " Delivered '' scan. 

I disputed this charge under Case ID XXXX. I provided Citi with irrefutable, official written evidence : a statement from the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( Service Request # XXXX ). This federal document explicitly confirms the tracking number provided by the merchant was for a package weighing exactly XXXX oz and was delivered to a different name and address. 

On XX/XX/year>, Citi denied my dispute and reversed my temporary credit, stating the " documentation or facts provided do not support the billing error dispute claim. '' Citi clearly relied solely on the merchant 's automated tracking link and completely ignored the federal XXXX documentation I provided. By ignoring direct proof of mail fraud from the United States Postal Service, Citi failed its legal obligation to conduct a reasonable and thorough investigation.
